agilitycompetition

Class Date

  • java.lang.Object
    • agilitycompetition.Date
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    java.lang.Comparable<Date>

    public class Date
extends java.lang.Object
implements java.lang.Comparable<Date>
    The Date Class represents a date as 3 int values for month, day, and year. Year is validated only as greater than 4712 BCE. Month is validated for values from 1..12. Day is validated based on the month that was set. dayNumber is the number of elapsed days since January 1, 4712 BCE Java provides several date classes that are more complicated than this one.
    Author:
    aapplin

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor and Description
      Date()
      Default Constructor Sets the date to January 1, 1970
      Date(int month, int day, int year)
      Parameterized Constructor Validates Year, Month, and Day.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Static Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      int compareTo(Date that)
      compareTo (abstract method of the Comparable Interface) is
      implemented to impose a natural ordering on a group of objects.
      compareTo is used by the Collections.sort routine to allow us to sort
      the Dog belonging to some Java collection by birthdate.
      double difference(Date that)
      calculates the difference between two dates as an absolute value
      of the two day numbers.
      int getDay()
      Accessor for the value of the property day.
      double getDayNumber()
      Accessor for dayNumber
      int getMonth()
      Accessor for the value of the property month.
      int getYear()
      Accessor for the value of the property year
      boolean isLeapYear()
      given that year is valid, this method check to see if it is a
      leap year.
      static void main(java.lang.String[] args)
      THe main method
      void setDay(int day)
      validates the day passed in from the constructor.
      void setMonth(int month)
      Validates the month passed in.
      java.lang.String toString()
      creates a string for date in the form mm/dd/yyyy

      • Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

        clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, wait, wait, wait

    • Constructor Detail

      • Date

        public Date()
        Default Constructor Sets the date to January 1, 1970

      • Date

        public Date(int month,
            int day,
            int year)
        Parameterized Constructor Validates Year, Month, and Day. Sets invalid values to 1/1/1970 Calls: calcDayNumber() to set that property.
        Parameters:
        month - input integer for month.
        day - input integer for day.
        year - input integer for year

    • Method Detail

      • setMonth

        public void setMonth(int month)
        Validates the month passed in. If the month is not valid it is set to 1.
        Parameters:
        month - the value sent in by the calling module

      • setDay

        public void setDay(int day)
        validates the day passed in from the constructor. Preconditions: The month must already be validated for this to work.
        Parameters:
        day - the value sent in by the calling module

      • isLeapYear

        public boolean isLeapYear()
        given that year is valid, this method check to see if it is a
        leap year. used to validate a day in February.
        Returns:
        true if the year is a leap year using standard algorithm.

      • difference

        public double difference(Date that)
        calculates the difference between two dates as an absolute value
        of the two day numbers.
        Parameters:
        that - a second date.
        Returns:
        the number of elapsed days.

      • getDay

        public int getDay()
        Accessor for the value of the property day.
        Returns:
        current value of the property day.

      • getMonth

        public int getMonth()
        Accessor for the value of the property month.
        Returns:
        the current value of the property month.

      • getYear

        public int getYear()
        Accessor for the value of the property year
        Returns:
        the current value of the property year.

      • getDayNumber

        public double getDayNumber()
        Accessor for dayNumber
        Returns:
        the current value of dayNumber

      • toString

        public java.lang.String toString()
        creates a string for date in the form mm/dd/yyyy
        Overrides:
        toString in class java.lang.Object
        Returns:
        a string in the form mm/dd/yyyy

      • compareTo

        public int compareTo(Date that)
        compareTo (abstract method of the Comparable Interface) is
        implemented to impose a natural ordering on a group of objects.
        compareTo is used by the Collections.sort routine to allow us to sort
        the Dog belonging to some Java collection by birthdate.
        Specified by:
        compareTo in interface java.lang.Comparable<Date>
        Parameters:
        that - is the Date object we are comparing this one to
        Returns:
        a negative integer, zero, or a positive integer if this object
        comes before, is equal to, or comes after the specified object.

      • main

        public static void main(java.lang.String[] args)
        THe main method
        Parameters:
        args - command line arguments
